name: Valle de Lana from Santa Cruz de Campezo – circular by the Vía Verde del FFCC Vasco-Navarro
name_original: Valle de Lana desde Santa Cruz de Campezo – circular por la Vía Verde del FFCC Vasco-Navarro
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Campezo / Kanpezu are set within the historical territory of Álava in the Basque Country, characterized by diverse landscapes. The region features the historic Vía Verde del FF.CC. Vasco-Navarro, which traverses valleys and mountains. Extensive forests, including marojal, beech, and holm oak, are found within Izki Natural Park, while the Codés Mountain Range contributes to the area's rugged topography. The Ega River also flows through the municipality, adding to the picturesque riverside scenery. Best touring cycling routes around Campezo / Kanpezu The most popular touring cycling route is Vasco-Navarro Greenway: Campezo Loop, a 21.0 miles (33.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route explores a historic greenway known for its gentle gradients. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valle de Lana from Santa Cruz de Campezo – circular by the Vía Verde del FFCC Vasco-Navarro, a difficult 25.1 miles (40.4 km) path. This route features varied landscapes and includes segments that may be unpaved. Local touring cyclists also love the Arquijas Tunnel – Arquijas Viaduct loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu, a 19.9 miles (32.1 km) trail leading through the Arquijas Tunnel and past the Arquijas Viaduct, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Campezo / Kanpezu is defined by the Vía Verde del FF.CC. Vasco-Navarro greenway, extensive forests of Izki Natural Park, and the rugged Codés Mountain Range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Campezo / Kanpezu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campezo / Kanpezu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: There are 18 touring cycling routes around Campezo / Kanpezu, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These include 3 moderate and 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: While most routes are rated moderate to difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079141202/vasco-navarro-greenway-campezo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Greenway: Campezo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, though classified as difficult due to its length, follows a historic railway line with gentle gradients, making it more accessible for those seeking a less strenuous ride, especially with electric bicycles. The greenway's smooth course through the Sierra de Codés is generally suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse diverse landscapes, including the extensive forests of Izki Natural Park, characterized by marojal, beech, and holm oak woodlands. You'll also encounter the rugged topography of the Codés Mountain Range and picturesque riverside scenery along the Ega River. The Vía Verde del FF.CC. Vasco-Navarro greenway itself offers a blend of nature and history, passing through valleys and mountains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The Vía Verde del FF.CC. Vasco-Navarro features restored original stations, tunnels, and bridges. Notably, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquijas Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical passage. You can also explore charming medieval villages like Santa Cruz de Campezo/Santikurutze Kanpezu with its 14th-century Gothic Church of Asunción de Nuestra Señora, and Antoñana, a National Monument Complex with typical medieval buildings and an Interpretation Center of the Vía Verde.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077709160/valle-de-lana-from-santa-cruz-de-campezo-circular-by-the-via-verde-del-ffcc-vasco-navarro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Lana from Santa Cruz de Campezo – circular by the Vía Verde del FFCC Vasco-Navarro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atauri Tunnel – Atauri Viaduct loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the historical Vía Verde del FF.CC. Vasco-Navarro greenway, and the extensive forests of Izki Natural Park, which offer a unique blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful waterfalls. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranbaltza Dam and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4387004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabando Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions offer scenic detours from your cycling adventure.

Q: Where can I rent a bicycle in the area?
A: Bicycle rental services are available in Antoñana, making it convenient for visitors to explore the area's touring cycling routes without bringing their own equipment.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: The diverse landscapes of Campezo / Kanpezu, including its forests and greenways, are enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer provides longer daylight hours. The Vía Verde's gentle gradients make it accessible for much of the year, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or mountain peaks accessible from the cycling routes?
A: While the touring routes themselves might not lead directly to the highest peaks, the region is surrounded by the Codés Mountain Range, offering stunning vistas. Notable summits include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3431427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costalera&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz – Arquijas Tunnel loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the Sierra de Lokiz.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with other outdoor activities?
A: Absolutely. Campezo / Kanpezu is an excellent base for combining cycling with other activities. Izki Natural Park offers 15 well-marked routes for hiking, and the Codés Mountain Range provides opportunities for mountaineering and birdwatching. The region's natural beauty and diverse terrain cater to a wide range of outdoor enthusiasts.
tips:
This Arquijas viaduct forms part of the greenway of the Basque-Navarrese railway.

A perfect gravel area for cycling. No traffic and surrounded by nature. A pleasant experience for the senses.

Here are the ruins of the old Zuñiga station, built in 1925. The station is the smallest of the stations on the Navarre section. It was third rate. On the ground floor there was an office, hall and warehouse. The upper house had a dining room, kitchen, three bedrooms and a WC. Next to the station is the goods dock and the warehouse.

It occupies a strategic location for the union of old paths: the one that from Treviño, along the bed of the Ayuda river, reached Estella and those that followed the courses of the Ega and Inglares rivers. You can visit the Church of the Assumption of Our Lady, the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Ibernalo, the stone cross, the Humilladero del Santo Cristo and the convent of San Juan de Piérola.
